metadata.seed: My pet died last week and I also failed my exam

patient.query: My pet died last week and I also failed my exam

entailment: Sudden bereavement due to life event (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear how much this has been weighing on you lately. It sounds like a lot to process all at once.

patient.query: I’ve been feeling really low lately. Some days it just feels like this heavy cloud is following me around. I try to shake it off, but it’s like I’m stuck in this cycle, and I don’t really know how to get out of it.

automatic_thoughts: I don’t really know how to get out of it

llm_response.response: It sounds like that heavy cloud is really weighing you down. When you say you're stuck in a cycle, what keeps you feeling like you're stuck in that pattern?

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel heavy. I guess it’s like I keep thinking about all the things I should be doing but can’t. And then I feel worse for not being able to do them, which just makes it harder to even try. It’s like I’m constantly disappointing myself.

automatic_thoughts: I should be doing all these things

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure from your own expectations. When you say you're disappointed in yourself, what impact does that self-criticism have on your motivation to move forward?

patient.query: It just makes everything feel more overwhelming, you know? Like, I already feel so low, and then on top of that, I’m berating myself for not being better or doing more. It’s like I’m in this tug-of-war with myself, and I end up just wanting to hide away instead of facing things.

automatic_thoughts: berating myself for not being better

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those conflicting thoughts about yourself are really adding to the weight you're already carrying. Can you tell me more about how that internal struggle affects your ability to move forward?

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely gets in the way. When I have those thoughts racing in my head, it’s hard to even get started on anything. I’ll sit there, just staring at a task, feeling like I’m frozen. And I worry that if I make a mistake or don’t do it perfectly, it just adds to everything I’m already feeling. So sometimes, I just don’t do anything at all.

llm_response.response: It sounds really frustrating when those thoughts make it hard to even begin tasks, and I hear how overwhelming the pressure for perfection can feel. That must be incredibly draining to carry around all that worry.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is draining. I want to do well, but the worry just takes over, and it feels like I’m juggling a million things at once. It’s like I can’t escape that inner voice telling me I won’t measure up. Sometimes, it gets so loud that I just want to shut everything out and not deal with it anymore.

automatic_thoughts: I can’t escape that inner voice

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure you're feeling is really weighing on you, even though you genuinely want to succeed. Can you tell me more about how that inner voice makes you feel, and what evidence you've found to support or challenge the idea that you won't measure up?

patient.query: It’s really draining. I want to do well, but the worry takes over, and it feels like I’m juggling a million things. I can’t escape the voice in my head telling me I won’t measure up. Sometimes, it gets so loud that I just want to shut everything out and not deal with it.

llm_response.response: It sounds like that internal voice is really taxing your energy, even though you're motivated to do well. Can you tell me more about what that voice might be trying to protect you from?

patient.query: I guess I’ve always felt pressure to be perfect, to not let anyone down. That voice just keeps reminding me of all the times I’ve messed up or failed. Maybe it thinks it’s keeping me safe from more disappointment or criticism, but instead, it just makes me feel worse. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where I can’t even try without that fear weighing me down.

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're noticing how that internal pressure to be perfect might be creating more distress than it's preventing. Can you tell me more about where you think that voice comes from and how it affects your ability to take risks or try new things?

patient.query: I guess it comes from a lot of places. Growing up, there was always this expectation to excel, to be the best at everything. I felt like I had to prove my worth, and if I didn’t, it meant I was failing somehow. That voice just feels like an echo of those expectations, and it makes me hesitant to even try new things. I worry that if I don't do it perfectly, I’ll disappoint myself and everyone else. So, I end up avoiding risks altogether,

automatic_thoughts: I have to prove my worth
If I don't succeed, I'm failing
I worry about not doing it perfectly

neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those early pressures have created a cycle where you feel you must always succeed to prove your worth. Can you tell me more about how those old messages still influence your decisions today?

patient.query: Yeah, it really does shape a lot of what I do. When I think about trying something new, that voice starts up right away, listing all the ways I could fail. It feels like I can’t just enjoy an experience; instead, I’m constantly grading myself on how well I’m doing. I end up overthinking everything, and sometimes I just back away instead of even giving it a shot. It’s exhausting, and I hate that it still holds so much power over me.

automatic_thoughts: listing all the ways I could fail
I can’t just enjoy an experience
constantly grading myself on how well I’m doing
behaviors: back away instead of giving it a shot

neutral: Able to resist dangerous thoughts with difficulty (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like that internal dialogue is really shaping your experience of new possibilities. When you mentioned feeling like you're constantly grading yourself, I wonder how that might affect your ability to just explore something without judgment.
